The diff adds a static UI summary card to a mockup file (`docs/vercel_mockup/src/MOCKUP.jsx`). Per the user's memory, this directory contains a Vercel mockup — purely presentational React JSX.

Analyzing the added code:
- All values are hardcoded literals: the count `14`, the labels `"Gov/Hospital walk-ins"`, `"Approved + pending · ₱0 (admin-approved walk-in)"`, and the style colors
- No user input, no props, no template interpolation, no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, no event handlers, no network calls
- No entry points and no sinks (shell/SQL/eval/fs/HTML-render/etc.) are touched
- The JSX renders only static text via React's normal escaped text nodes

There is no attacker-influenceable data flow introduced. No vulnerabilities to report.
